title: Sediment Transport Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Sediment Transport Inversion: Sediment transport inversion: infer bed material size distribution and critical shear stress from bedload and suspended transport measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.mpm.bedload_formula…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~40; armoring_effect, wash_load_contamination set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: q_b = 8*(tau* - tau*_c) ** 1.5 * sqrt((s-1)*g*d ** 3) (MPM); q_s = c_a * u * h (van Rijn suspended)
E.world_state_x: d50 bed material grain size (mm), tau*_c critical Shields stress, bed roughness ks
E.observation_y: bedload flux q_b (kg/m/s), suspended sediment concentration SSC (mg/L) vs. discharge

W.regime: Existence of the recovered sediment_flux_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 40); armoring_effect dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: bedload flux within factor 2 for well-constrained conditions. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~40. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |armoring_effect|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_measurements) for formula-based inversion per iteration
